item.title: Wall Street Embraces the Dollar as Warsh’s Fed Activates Bulls
item.description: The dollar is wrapping up one of its best months in a year as a raft of Wall Street banks see a turnaround of fortunes for the US currency.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain as Traders Weigh Hormuz Flows After Ship Attack
item.description: Oil held a gain as traders weighed the outlook for a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz after a cargo ship was attacked in the waterway.

item.title: Asia Stocks Set for Choppy Open on Tech Volatility: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia were set for a mixed open Friday after a volatile Wall Street session, where renewed selling in megacap technology shares offset gains fueled by resilient US economic data.

item.title: Trump Administration Asks OpenAI to Stagger Release of AI Model
item.description: The Trump administration has asked OpenAI to stagger the release of an upcoming powerful artificial intelligence model, according to a person familiar with the matter, nearly two weeks after rival Anthropic PBC suspended its most capable offerings from the market under regulatory pressure.

item.title: Permira-Backed Womenswear Retailer Reformation Files for US IPO
item.description: Reformation Inc., a sustainable womenswear brand that’s backed by private equity firm Permira, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing growing sales.

item.title: South Korea’s ‘Ant’ Army Is Driving an AI Market Frenzy
item.description: Some 14 million retail investors are piling into shares of two big tech giants, but worries are growing about what happens if chip demand dries up.

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: ホルムズ海峡で船舶に攻撃、米PCE価格と消費支出、アップル値上げ、JPモルガンCEO後任争いで新局面、自動車部品マレリの資産取得協議

item.title: Trump-Backed Golf Resort Advances as Vietnam Seizes Farmland
item.description: Eviction notices have been sent to farmers in northern Vietnam to make way for a $1.5 billion Trump Organization-branded luxury golf resort project that’s already behind schedule.

item.title: UK’s Crown Estate Profits Fall as Offshore Wind Fees Decline
item.description: Profits at the Crown Estate, the property company that generates income for King Charles III, fell last year as a temporary boost from the licensing of new offshore wind farms began to fade.

item.title: Pentagon Sees Broader Role for AI in Setting Military Targets
item.description: The Pentagon has quietly revised its doctrine on how the US military picks its targets in battle, opening the way for artificial intelligence to make critical wartime decisions in the future.

item.title: OpenAI Leans Toward Waiting Until 2027 for IPO, NYT Says
item.description: OpenAI is leaning toward holding off on an initial public offering until 2027, the New York Times reported, citing three people involved in the company’s deliberations.

item.title: Chipmaker Onsemi to Buy Synaptics in $6 Billion Stock Deal
item.description: US chipmaker Onsemi has agreed to buy Synaptics Inc., a firm that specializes in semiconductors for smart devices, in an all-stock deal that values the company at about $6.2 billion.

item.title: Apple Podcasts’ Video Push Shows Early Promise
item.description: Apple charged into video podcasts earlier this year after ceding market share to YouTube. Shows that opted in early are cautiously optimistic about the results.

item.title: Trump to Pick Telecom Lawyer to Lead DOJ’s Antitrust Division
item.description: The White House is poised to nominate Adam Candeub, the general counsel at the Federal Communications Commission, to take over as the Justice Department’s antitrust chief, according to people familiar with the plans.

item.title: US Invests $250 Million in Billionaire’s Startup for Chip Making
item.description: I-Pulse Inc. will receive $250 million in US funding for semiconductor and pulsed-power development as the venture co-founded by Robert Friedland joins American efforts to reduce reliance on foreign-chip supply chains.

item.title: Would Congress’ New Housing Bill Lower Home Prices?
item.description: President Donald Trump’s decision to back out of signing a major housing bill provided a dramatic last-minute twist to lawmakers’ efforts to address Americans’ cost-of-living concerns. The 21st Century Road to Housing Act, which passed both chambers of Congress by a huge margin, is designed to spur housing construction and, in the long run, bring relief to consumers struggling with lofty prices and mortgage rates.

item.title: Mexico Holds Key Rate, Pledges to Keep It at 6.5% for Longer
item.description: Mexico’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate unchanged and signaled it will keep it at the current level for longer as it gages the impact of recent price shocks on inflation.

item.title: Bank of Canada Urged to Use Fewer Core Gauges, Focus on Headline Inflation
item.description: The Bank of Canada is being urged to focus on headline inflation instead of abstracting to core measures when discussing their policy decisions as it sought feedback on its mandate renewal.

item.title: Endless Shrimp Deal Was Scheme to Squeeze Red Lobster, Suit Says
item.description: Red Lobster’s $20 all-you-can-eat shrimp deal wasn’t just a badly designed promotion that inadvertently helped tip the iconic American restaurant chain into bankruptcy.

item.title: More People Worked From Home in 2025 Despite RTO Mandates
item.description: More US employees worked from home in 2025 than the year prior, a small but notable shift that suggests companies’ years-long efforts to get workers back into the office have hit a wall.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Sees Glimmers of Hope In Latest Inflation Report
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said he saw hopeful signs in the latest inflation report, but warned that price pressures overall remain too high.

item.title: EU Extends Tariff Suspension in Boeing-Airbus Trade Dispute
item.description: European Union member states agreed to extend the suspension of tariffs set to hit $4 billion of American products related to a trade dispute between Airbus SE and Boeing Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: NYC ‘Classical Christian’ School Wins UWS Campus for $33 Million
item.description: Geneva School of Manhattan — an institution that describes itself as a “classical Christian school” — is acquiring the former Upper West Side home of a bankrupt private school for $33.2 million.

item.title: Waymo Registers German Entity for Future European Expansion
item.description: Waymo is planning to offer its driverless robotaxis in Germany, the latest move by the Alphabet Inc. unit to expand outside the US.

item.title: Fire Breaks Out at Delta Air Lines Refinery in Pennsylvania
item.description: A fire broke out at Delta Air Lines Inc.’s refinery in Trainer, Pennsylvania, on Thursday.

item.title: Stellantis, Nissan in Talks to Buy Assets From Troubled Marelli
item.description: Stellantis NV and Nissan Motor Co. are in talks to take over some of the assets of Marelli Holdings Co., the global auto-parts maker that’s mired in a challenging reorganization in bankruptcy.

item.title: Hertz Heads for Record Weekly Drop on Funding Plan, Profit Guide
item.description: A record-setting rout in Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock is continuing for a second day.

item.title: Microsoft Raises Xbox Console Prices for Third Time in 13 Months
item.description: Microsoft Corp. announced a third substantial price increase for the company’s current-generation Xbox video-game consoles in a glaring example of the component shortage crisis that has universally driven up the cost of consumer tech products.

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Misfiring Funding Model Jolts Bitcoin Traders
item.description: Fears that Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin buying machine is beginning to seize up are spilling across the crypto market, fueling the latest leg of Bitcoin’s selloff and exposing cracks in one of the financial structures that has underpinned demand for the world’s largest cryptocurrency over the past two years.

item.title: Macflation Is Officially Here
item.description: Gadget prices inflate.
